But here's the thing: A therapist shouldn't have to be a social media expert. A wedding planner shouldn't spend 6 hours a week posting content. A wellness coach shouldn't hate their own marketing.

The problem isn't that they're bad at marketing. The problem is that they're doing marketing at all.

𝐀𝐭 𝐎𝐦𝐚𝐝𝐚, 𝐰𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐯𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐢𝐬 𝐢𝐧𝐯𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐦𝐚𝐫𝐤𝐞𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠.
Omada means "team" in Greek. Not a tool. Not a list. A team.
We've built the first AI marketing team specifically for service businesses - a "do it for me" team that creates, executes, optimizes, and supports while you focus on what you do best: serving clients.

For small business owners, it means you're never alone. While you build your business, your AI marketing team is already at work.

𝐓𝐡𝐨𝐮𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐚𝐧𝐢𝐞𝐬 𝐚𝐫𝐞 𝐚𝐥𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐲 𝐰𝐚𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐟𝐨𝐫 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐟𝐮𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞.
Technology should do what technology does best, so humans can focus on what humans do best.

That's not optimization. That's revolution.
𝐎𝐦𝐚𝐝𝐚. 𝐘𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐭𝐞𝐚𝐦, 𝐢𝐧 𝐦𝐨𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧.

title: Field Sales Representative
description_text:
Omada.ai is hiring ambitious field sales reps to introduce local businesses to our AI marketing platform. This isn't just another commission-only gig—we pay you $25/hour guaranteed ($1,000/week at 40 hours) PLUS $200 commission for every customer you close.
We're looking for people who can walk into a local business, start a conversation, and close deals. If you're ready to earn serious income while building real sales skills, this is your shot.
What You'll Do:
Walk into local businesses (salons, restaurants, gyms, coffee shops, pet stores) in your area and show them how Omada.ai can handle their marketing while they focus on running their business.
Your day looks like this:
Map your route – pick 10-20 businesses in a walkable area
Walk in and start the conversation – "Hi, I'm with Omada.ai. Who handles your social media?"
Show a 10-minute demo right there on your phone/tablet
Offer a 14-day free trial – no risk for them
Follow up a few days later – when they convert, you earn $200
Most reps close their first deal within the first week.
What You'll Earn:
Base: $25/hour ($1,000/week full-time)
Commission: $200 per closed customer
Payment: Bi-monthly via direct deposit
Real Income Potential:
5 customers/month: $5,000/month ($4K base + $1K commission)
10 customers/month: $6,000/month ($4K base + $2K commission)
20 customers/month: $8,000/month ($4K base + $4K commission)
Top performers: $10K+/month
Commission is uncapped. The more you close, the more you make.
What We're Looking For:
✅ Comfortable talking to people – You're not afraid to walk into a business and start a conversation
✅ Willing to hustle – You understand that sales is about volume and consistency
✅ Coachable – You'll follow our proven system and improve every week
✅ Reliable – You show up, work your territory, and follow through
✅ Self-motivated – You don't need someone looking over your shoulder
Bonus if you have:
✓ Sales experience (insurance, real estate, merchant services, B2B)
✓ Experience working with small business owners
✓ Your own transportation or live in a walkable area with high business density
No degree required. No sales experience required. If you can hold a conversation and you're willing to work, we'll teach you everything else.
About Omada.ai
We're an AI-powered marketing platform built specifically for small businesses. We handle social media, email marketing, ads, and reputation management—so business owners can stop being reluctant marketers and get back to what they do best.
We're venture-backed, growing fast, and building a team of sales reps who want to win.
This Role Is Perfect For:
Career changers exploring sales with guaranteed income
College grads who want to build real skills and real money
Experienced sales reps looking for better comp structure
Hustlers who know they can sell but need the right opportunity
Anyone tired of purely commission-based work but still wants upside
Ready to Start?
Apply now. We're hiring in select cities and looking for people who are ready to get started immediately.
Must reside in the United States
Phone/laptop/tablet and internet access
Ability to commit 40 hours per week
Reliable transportation or walkable territory
Comfortable working independently
Equal opportunity employer.
1099 independent contractor position.
$25/hour base + $200 commission per closed customer.
ai_core_responsibilities: The representative will introduce local businesses to the Omada.ai AI marketing platform by mapping out walkable territories, entering businesses, starting conversations, and providing a brief demonstration. Responsibilities include offering a 14-day free trial and following up to convert prospects into paying customers.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must be comfortable initiating conversations with strangers, willing to maintain high sales volume and consistency, coachable to follow a proven system, reliable in executing territory work, and self-motivated to work independently. No degree or prior sales experience is required; the company will provide training.

title: Site Supervisor - Water and Waste
locations_derived: Ballina Shire Council, New South Wales, Australia
description_text:
Join Alder Group - Celebrating 22 Years of Excellence in 2025!
Are you ready to lead high-impact water and wastewater infrastructure projects across some of the most scenic regions in Southeast Queensland and Northern Rivers NSW? Alder Group is expanding, and we’re seeking experienced Construction Supervisors with a strong background in civil infrastructure, particularly in water and wastewater works.
This role may involve Drive-In Drive-Out (DIDO) arrangements depending on project location, so flexibility and a willingness to travel are essential.
About the role
The duties of the supervisor roles will be refined for the various level of supervisor; the general expectations will be:
Conduct site inspections to ensure quality and safety outcomes.
Coordinate subcontractors and manage site teams including labour and supervision.
Work autonomously and collaboratively to deliver projects on time and within budget.
Maintain daily records, assist with cost control, and track productivity.
Liaise professionally with clients and stakeholders to uphold Alder’s reputation.
Ensure WHS and environmental controls are implemented and monitored.
Review subcontractor scopes and manage defect rectification.
Participate in lessons learned sessions to drive continuous improvement.
About You - Skills and Experience
Minimum 3+ years’ experience supervising water and wastewater infrastructure projects.
Demonstrates strong leadership in managing construction of treatment plants, pump stations, pipelines, reservoirs, and associated civil works.
Can confidently interpret and work from hydraulic, structural, and civil drawings and specifications.
Has a solid understanding of water industry standards, environmental compliance, and safety protocols.
Is proactive, quality-driven, and focused on delivering outcomes within budget and timeframes.
Communicates effectively with clients, subcontractors, and internal teams.
Comfortable using mobile apps and construction software (e.g., Procore) for daily reporting and coordination.
Why Join Us: If you are looking for a role that offers excitement, stability, flexibility, and exposure to diverse projects, Alder Group is the place for you! 
The Civil and Infrastructure team work on a diverse range of projects of values ranging from $1m to $30m. We have capabilities across all facets of civil and infrastructure, from earthworks, road and bridge construction, theme park upgrades, water and wastewater treatment plants, pump stations, reservoirs, subdivisions and concrete remediation.
Each project showcases our innovative construction methods and problem-solving mindset, and we’re committed to delivering excellence while empowering our team to reach their full potential. At Alder, you’re not just joining a company—you’re becoming part of a team that values collaboration, integrity, and pride in everything we do. 
What We Offer:
· Competitive remuneration package.
· Supportive structure and workplace culture.
· Professional challenges that stimulate growth and prosperity for all.
· We live by our values of TEAMWORK, PARTNERSHIPS, INTEGRITY, and PRIDE.
ai_benefits: Professional challenges that stimulate growth and prosperity
ai_remote_location: Ballina Shire Council, New South Wales, Australia
ai_remote_location_derived: Ballina Shire Council, New South Wales, Australia
ai_core_responsibilities: The supervisor will conduct site inspections to ensure quality and safety outcomes while coordinating subcontractors and managing site teams to deliver projects on time and within budget. Key duties also include maintaining daily records, assisting with cost control, liaising with stakeholders, and ensuring WHS and environmental controls are strictly implemented.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a minimum of three years of experience supervising water and wastewater infrastructure projects, including treatment plants, pump stations, and pipelines. Essential requirements include the ability to interpret civil/structural drawings, a solid understanding of water industry standards, and effective communication skills.
linkedin_org_slogan: Alder Group offers a comprehensive end-to-end solution for your design, development, civil and construction needs.
linkedin_org_description:
Since its inception 20 years ago, Alder Group has quietly emerged as an industry force to be reckoned with.

Beginning with just one person who dreamt of a working environment where staff didn’t feel like a cog in a wheel and client relationships were built on integrity and respect, the company has grown into a multi-disciplined construction and development syndicate with an expanding geographical footprint. 

From our small beginnings we have continued to build strong, long-lasting relationships within our networks, taking pride in delivering outcomes that live up to our name. Where most construction or development companies niche themselves, we’ve always thrived on diversity – embracing civil and building projects with an ease that belies the complexities and developing multifaceted projects that offer real value to the community. Our multidisciplinary approach along with our ability to adapt to changing market conditions is what has set us apart from our competitors from the outset. And, with a long list of successful developments behind us, we have additional skillsets and expertise that other players do not.

With work that speaks for itself, it’s our thriving culture, professional edge and unwillingness to compromise on quality that truly underpins our success.

Development  |  Construction  |  Civil & Infrastructure

We leverage the synergy between Alder Developments and Alder Constructions to provide high quality customised solutions.

title: Mid to Senior Automation Tester C / Selenium
locations_alt_raw: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
description_text:
Mid to Senior Automation Tester (C# / Selenium)

Location:
- Central Kuala Lumpur, with MRT / LRT connection

Work Arrangement:
- Hybrid model: 3 days a week in the office

Why You Should Apply:
- European management style and culture; No fire-fighting, no overtime & contribute your ideas
- Exposure to the newest technology and engineering best practices; excellent learning environment
- Reputable company with USD 400 million in new funding, experiencing fast growth and serving “blue chip” enterprise customers

Key Responsibilities:
- Advocate best practices in testing to foster a quality-first mindset across the team
- Enable Developers to contribute to automated scripts
- Perform testing activities, with approximately 80% automation and 20% manual testing
- Develop and implement reliable testing strategies
- Identify process or technical gaps and propose solutions for improvement

Requirements:
- 3 to 8 years of automation testing experience
- Early career experience as a .NET / C# Engineer or recent C# testing experience

About the Company & Team:
- European technology “unicorn” with a data-driven and AI-enabled SaaS product for enterprise clients
- Serving 100,000 companies across 175+ countries
- New technology hub in Kuala Lumpur with 50+ people, established in 2024

How to Apply:
- Click the “apply” button
- You may submit an old CV or your LinkedIn profile if preferred
- The recruiter (Graham) is available to provide more information
- Every applicant or enquiry will receive a reply

Additional Notes:
- Employment passes, work visas, or relocation support are not provided
- Only applicants with work rights in Malaysia will be considered
ai_core_responsibilities: The role involves advocating for best practices in testing to promote a quality-first mindset and enabling developers to contribute to automated scripts. Responsibilities also include performing testing activities, with a focus on 80% automation and 20% manual testing, and developing reliable testing strategies.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess 3 to 8 years of automation testing experience, ideally including early career experience as a .NET/C# Engineer or recent C# testing experience. Applicants must have existing work rights in Malaysia, as no visa sponsorship is provided.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Greece)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Greece.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

This role requires collaboration with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting and strategic decision-making.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, with proficiency in SQL and experience in building dashboards. Strong analytical skills and the ability to communicate insights effectively are essential.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Turkey)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Turkey.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether?

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Sweden)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Sweden.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Singapore)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Singapore.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Netherlands)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Netherlands.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth in Netherlands.
This role leads two critical engineering squads focused on Dashboard and Growth, driving both product adoption and customer success. You will combine hands-on technical expertise with strategic leadership to influence product direction, ensure scalable architecture, and deliver high-impact features. The position requires mentoring engineers, balancing urgency with quality, and partnering with product, design, and GTM teams to turn vision into reality. You will oversee onboarding flows, analytics dashboards, and other growth initiatives that directly affect customer engagement and satisfaction. This is an ideal role for a seasoned engineering leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and enjoys shaping both team culture and technical outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Lead, mentor, and grow engineers across Dashboard and Growth squads, fostering high performance and technical excellence.
Translate product vision into actionable, high-quality deliverables while balancing scale, reliability, and performance.
Provide architectural guidance and participate in technical decision-making, ensuring clarity and maintainable solutions.
Drive teams to operate with focus, speed, and intentional trade-offs, while upholding high execution standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, and GTM teams to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.
Oversee development of onboarding flows, feature discovery, engagement tools, and analytics dashboards that enhance user value.
Monitor team performance metrics and iterate on processes to continuously improve delivery, predictability, and quality.
Requirements:
5+ years managing and scaling agile software engineering teams with proven experience in developing engineering talent.
10+ years building large-scale web applications, preferably in SaaS, with strong cloud, distributed systems, and customer-facing experience.
Hands-on technical expertise to assess architectural trade-offs, delivery risks, and influence team decisions.
Experience driving teams to deliver high-quality features predictably, improving velocity over time.
Strong product sensibility with experience in product discovery, delivery, and iterative improvement.
Outstanding communication skills to align teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Growth mindset with ability to adapt to ambiguity, pivot when necessary, and motivate teams to own outcomes.
Preferred: familiarity with Go, TypeScript/React, backend APIs, data-intensive dashboards, PLG initiatives, and SaaS security/privacy frameworks.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $177,000 – $235,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Bonus and stock options.
Fully remote work environment with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
401(k) retirement plan.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Opportunities for career growth and technical leadership.

\n

title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from France)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in France.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth in France.
This role leads two critical engineering squads focused on Dashboard and Growth, driving both product adoption and customer success. You will combine hands-on technical expertise with strategic leadership to influence product direction, ensure scalable architecture, and deliver high-impact features. The position requires mentoring engineers, balancing urgency with quality, and partnering with product, design, and GTM teams to turn vision into reality. You will oversee onboarding flows, analytics dashboards, and other growth initiatives that directly affect customer engagement and satisfaction. This is an ideal role for a seasoned engineering leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and enjoys shaping both team culture and technical outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Lead, mentor, and grow engineers across Dashboard and Growth squads, fostering high performance and technical excellence.
Translate product vision into actionable, high-quality deliverables while balancing scale, reliability, and performance.
Provide architectural guidance and participate in technical decision-making, ensuring clarity and maintainable solutions.
Drive teams to operate with focus, speed, and intentional trade-offs, while upholding high execution standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, and GTM teams to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.
Oversee development of onboarding flows, feature discovery, engagement tools, and analytics dashboards that enhance user value.
Monitor team performance metrics and iterate on processes to continuously improve delivery, predictability, and quality.
Requirements:
5+ years managing and scaling agile software engineering teams with proven experience in developing engineering talent.
10+ years building large-scale web applications, preferably in SaaS, with strong cloud, distributed systems, and customer-facing experience.
Hands-on technical expertise to assess architectural trade-offs, delivery risks, and influence team decisions.
Experience driving teams to deliver high-quality features predictably, improving velocity over time.
Strong product sensibility with experience in product discovery, delivery, and iterative improvement.
Outstanding communication skills to align teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Growth mindset with ability to adapt to ambiguity, pivot when necessary, and motivate teams to own outcomes.
Preferred: familiarity with Go, TypeScript/React, backend APIs, data-intensive dashboards, PLG initiatives, and SaaS security/privacy frameworks.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $177,000 – $235,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Bonus and stock options.
Fully remote work environment with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
401(k) retirement plan.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Opportunities for career growth and technical leadership.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. title: Revenue Operations Analyst (Remote from Belgium)
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst in Belgium.
This role is a key analytical position supporting Sales, Marketing, and Revenue leadership by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. You will own reporting, build dashboards, and analyze the full buyer journey to uncover trends, identify growth opportunities, and improve operational efficiency. The position requires collaboration with GTM and Finance teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and strategic decision-making. You will work with data across CRM, marketing, and analytics systems to ensure accuracy and reliability. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, strategy, and execution in a fast-paced SaaS environment, delivering insights that directly influence growth and revenue outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Develop, maintain, and automate dashboards and KPI reporting to provide reliable, self-serve insights for GTM teams.
Analyze sales, marketing, and operational data to identify trends, performance gaps, and opportunities across the buyer journey.
Perform CAC and ROAS analysis for acquisition channels to optimize spend efficiency and growth strategies.
Partner with Finance and Revenue teams to support forecasting, budgeting, and planning through data modeling and historical trend analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and governance across CRM, marketing, and analytics platforms.
Design and maintain win/loss analysis frameworks to capture feedback and inform improvements across Sales, Marketing, and Product.
Translate complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Requirements:
3-5 years of experience in an analytical role within a SaaS or high-growth tech company, or transferable experience from Consulting, Strategic Finance, Investment Banking, or Corporate Strategy.
Proficiency in SQL and solid understanding of data modeling concepts.
Experience building dashboards and visualizations in tools such as Sigma, Looker, Tableau, or Excel.
Familiarity with CRM systems and core GTM/revenue operations workflows.
Strong analytical instincts and ability to work with large, complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate analysis into compelling insights.
Detail-oriented problem solver with high standards for accuracy and data quality.
Thrives in a fast-paced, evolving environment with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.
Comfortable working fully remotely using tools like Slack, Google Workspace, and Zoom.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $115,000 – $190,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Fully remote work with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
Opportunity to work on high-impact projects in a fast-growing SaaS company.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Career growth opportunities with exposure to cross-functional teams and strategic decision-making.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth in Czech Republic.
This role leads two critical engineering squads focused on Dashboard and Growth, driving both product adoption and customer success. You will combine hands-on technical expertise with strategic leadership to influence product direction, ensure scalable architecture, and deliver high-impact features. The position requires mentoring engineers, balancing urgency with quality, and partnering with product, design, and GTM teams to turn vision into reality. You will oversee onboarding flows, analytics dashboards, and other growth initiatives that directly affect customer engagement and satisfaction. This is an ideal role for a seasoned engineering leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and enjoys shaping both team culture and technical outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Lead, mentor, and grow engineers across Dashboard and Growth squads, fostering high performance and technical excellence.
Translate product vision into actionable, high-quality deliverables while balancing scale, reliability, and performance.
Provide architectural guidance and participate in technical decision-making, ensuring clarity and maintainable solutions.
Drive teams to operate with focus, speed, and intentional trade-offs, while upholding high execution standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, and GTM teams to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.
Oversee development of onboarding flows, feature discovery, engagement tools, and analytics dashboards that enhance user value.
Monitor team performance metrics and iterate on processes to continuously improve delivery, predictability, and quality.
Requirements:
5+ years managing and scaling agile software engineering teams with proven experience in developing engineering talent.
10+ years building large-scale web applications, preferably in SaaS, with strong cloud, distributed systems, and customer-facing experience.
Hands-on technical expertise to assess architectural trade-offs, delivery risks, and influence team decisions.
Experience driving teams to deliver high-quality features predictably, improving velocity over time.
Strong product sensibility with experience in product discovery, delivery, and iterative improvement.
Outstanding communication skills to align teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Growth mindset with ability to adapt to ambiguity, pivot when necessary, and motivate teams to own outcomes.
Preferred: familiarity with Go, TypeScript/React, backend APIs, data-intensive dashboards, PLG initiatives, and SaaS security/privacy frameworks.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $177,000 – $235,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Bonus and stock options.
Fully remote work environment with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
401(k) retirement plan.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Opportunities for career growth and technical leadership.

\n

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth
description_text:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth in South Africa.
This role leads two critical engineering squads focused on Dashboard and Growth, driving both product adoption and customer success. You will combine hands-on technical expertise with strategic leadership to influence product direction, ensure scalable architecture, and deliver high-impact features. The position requires mentoring engineers, balancing urgency with quality, and partnering with product, design, and GTM teams to turn vision into reality. You will oversee onboarding flows, analytics dashboards, and other growth initiatives that directly affect customer engagement and satisfaction. This is an ideal role for a seasoned engineering leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and enjoys shaping both team culture and technical outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Lead, mentor, and grow engineers across Dashboard and Growth squads, fostering high performance and technical excellence.
Translate product vision into actionable, high-quality deliverables while balancing scale, reliability, and performance.
Provide architectural guidance and participate in technical decision-making, ensuring clarity and maintainable solutions.
Drive teams to operate with focus, speed, and intentional trade-offs, while upholding high execution standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, and GTM teams to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.
Oversee development of onboarding flows, feature discovery, engagement tools, and analytics dashboards that enhance user value.
Monitor team performance metrics and iterate on processes to continuously improve delivery, predictability, and quality.
Requirements:
5+ years managing and scaling agile software engineering teams with proven experience in developing engineering talent.
10+ years building large-scale web applications, preferably in SaaS, with strong cloud, distributed systems, and customer-facing experience.
Hands-on technical expertise to assess architectural trade-offs, delivery risks, and influence team decisions.
Experience driving teams to deliver high-quality features predictably, improving velocity over time.
Strong product sensibility with experience in product discovery, delivery, and iterative improvement.
Outstanding communication skills to align teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Growth mindset with ability to adapt to ambiguity, pivot when necessary, and motivate teams to own outcomes.
Preferred: familiarity with Go, TypeScript/React, backend APIs, data-intensive dashboards, PLG initiatives, and SaaS security/privacy frameworks.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $177,000 – $235,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Bonus and stock options.
Fully remote work environment with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
401(k) retirement plan.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Opportunities for career growth and technical leadership.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether?

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Engineering Manager, Dashboard + Growth in Ireland.
This role leads two critical engineering squads focused on Dashboard and Growth, driving both product adoption and customer success. You will combine hands-on technical expertise with strategic leadership to influence product direction, ensure scalable architecture, and deliver high-impact features. The position requires mentoring engineers, balancing urgency with quality, and partnering with product, design, and GTM teams to turn vision into reality. You will oversee onboarding flows, analytics dashboards, and other growth initiatives that directly affect customer engagement and satisfaction. This is an ideal role for a seasoned engineering leader who thrives in fast-paced, high-growth environments and enjoys shaping both team culture and technical outcomes.
\n

Accountabilities:
Lead, mentor, and grow engineers across Dashboard and Growth squads, fostering high performance and technical excellence.
Translate product vision into actionable, high-quality deliverables while balancing scale, reliability, and performance.
Provide architectural guidance and participate in technical decision-making, ensuring clarity and maintainable solutions.
Drive teams to operate with focus, speed, and intentional trade-offs, while upholding high execution standards.
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Design, and GTM teams to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.
Oversee development of onboarding flows, feature discovery, engagement tools, and analytics dashboards that enhance user value.
Monitor team performance metrics and iterate on processes to continuously improve delivery, predictability, and quality.
Requirements:
5+ years managing and scaling agile software engineering teams with proven experience in developing engineering talent.
10+ years building large-scale web applications, preferably in SaaS, with strong cloud, distributed systems, and customer-facing experience.
Hands-on technical expertise to assess architectural trade-offs, delivery risks, and influence team decisions.
Experience driving teams to deliver high-quality features predictably, improving velocity over time.
Strong product sensibility with experience in product discovery, delivery, and iterative improvement.
Outstanding communication skills to align teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Growth mindset with ability to adapt to ambiguity, pivot when necessary, and motivate teams to own outcomes.
Preferred: familiarity with Go, TypeScript/React, backend APIs, data-intensive dashboards, PLG initiatives, and SaaS security/privacy frameworks.
Benefits:
Competitive salary range: $177,000 – $235,000 USD, based on experience and location.
Bonus and stock options.
Fully remote work environment with flexible schedule.
Health, dental, and vision benefits.
401(k) retirement plan.
Collaborative, inclusive, and supportive company culture.
Opportunities for career growth and technical leadership.

\n
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether? 

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

title: Test Operator
description_text:
Who We Are:
Venus Aerospace is revolutionizing rocket engine propulsion. With the first generational leap in rocket engine technology since Apollo, our proprietary Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine and Venus Detonation Ramjets deliver historic efficiency and performance across aerospace applications. We are building the next generation of propulsion platforms that enable breakthrough capabilities in space, defense, and commercial high-speed flight. Our team thrives on solving the toughest engineering challenges and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in aerospace. At Venus, we’re looking for bold engineers and innovators who want to make history and redefine the future of propulsion.

The Role: Test Operator
As a Test Operator, you will be at the center of propulsion and systems testing, ensuring safe, efficient, and reliable operations. You’ll collaborate across disciplines and drive continuous improvements in test execution.
\n

What You'll Get To Do
Coordinate and execute test campaigns, including propulsion hot fires, structural, vibration, and thermal testing.
Operate and monitor test stand systems, propulsion hardware, and instrumentation.
Lead the execution of test procedures, ensuring compliance with engineering requirements and safety standards.
Author, review, and refine detailed test procedures and analyze test data for accuracy and performance.
Collaborate with technicians to troubleshoot instrumentation issues and improve reliability.
Support setup, upgrades, maintenance, and breakdown of test stands to ensure readiness for future campaigns.
Act as the key liaison between test, propulsion, manufacturing, and technician teams to ensure smooth test stand operations.
Implement and maintain end-to-end data acquisition systems by authoring and integrating software code, developing the graphical user interface (GUI), and verifying instrument fidelity at company test sites to successfully operate systems and collect performance data.
Analyze test data in real time using engineering tools and scripts for visualization and evaluation
Collaborate with engineering and operations teams to improve hardware configurations, data collection tools and methods, and modify procedures to enhance overall test performance and reliability.
Work with test technicians and operations teams to continuously improve personnel and hardware safety at the test site through planning and implementation of operational procedures.
Monitor hardware and instrumentation at the test site and troubleshoot as needed to maintain or improve data fidelity.

What You Have
1 - 3 years of experience in a hardware testing environment. (Nationally recognized rocket club experience is accepted as qualifying hands-on experience)
Hands-on knowledge of test components: valves, pneumatic actuators/solenoids, limit switches, relief valves, pressure transducers, thermocouples, pressure vessels, GUIs/HMIs, and data acquisition systems.
Ability to read P&IDs and understand the flow of gases and fluids
Capable of performing basic instrumentation tasks, including wire cutting, crimping, and soldering
Ability to read and follow engineering instructions and independently implement new tools or data collection methods
Strong problem-solving skills, organizational abilities, and attention to detail.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite or equivalent software.
Clear communication skills, including over comms devices while operating consoles (computer workstation).
Ability to thrive both as part of a collaborative team and as a self-driven individual.
Comfortable working outdoors as needed; able to lift up to 25 lbs; capable of extended desk/computer work.

What Will Set You Apart
Experience with mechanical and fluid system design.
Experience with electrical and mechanical fabrication.
Experience with pressurized gases and liquids up to 6000psia.
Experience with NI hardware (PXIe, cRIO, cDAQ).
Experience designing and commissioning new test facilities.
Experience with GIT repositories and/or JSON file types.
Experience with LabVIEW 
Experience with Vizio
Experience with python or MATLAB
Experience with Confluence or Jira

\n
Location:
On-site in Houston, TX

Benefits:
Venus Aerospace employee benefits include a 401(k) plan, stock options, and medical benefits, including dental and vision. We have a flex-Friday flexible schedule, unlimited PTO, and receive professional development opportunities.

About Venus Aerospace
By enabling one hour global travel, Venus Aerospace is transforming the world and our access to it. We’re building a spaceplane with a low carbon footprint that will enable one-hour global travel. We are a diverse, veteran team with extreme experience in successful new space, commercial, R&D, and government/military programs.
We are looking for incredible, motivated, enthusiastic people that want to build a world-changing culture and organization. You will be helping Venus grow and develop, so flexibility and a sense of adventure is quintessential. We are looking for all-around AWESOME people to help us grow.
We’ve done the professional grind, and we’ve got a better approach. We take “Home for Dinner” seriously – we want you to work for us and be “home for dinner”. Family is important and we don’t want you to miss out on what matters most. If that appeals to you, we’d love to connect!
ITAR Requirements
This position involves access to technology that is subject to U.S. export controls. Any job offer made will be contingent upon the applicant’s capacity to serve in compliance with U.S. export controls. To conform to U.S. Government space technology export regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) you must be able to meet ITAR requirements (U.S. citizen, lawful permanent resident of the U.S., protected individual as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State). You can learn more about ITAR here.
Venus Aerospace is an Equal Opportunity Employer including Disabled/Veterans. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability. If you have a disability and believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please email careerassistance@venusaero.com or call (281) 203-0027.This email and phone number were created exclusively to assist job seekers whose disability prevents them from being able to apply online. Messages or any other purposes, such as following up on an application, third party staffing agencies, website technical issues, or any other issues not related to a disability, will not receive a response. Messages will be returned within five business days.
For additional information about our Equal Opportunity Employment Policy, please visit our EEOP page.

title: Senior Avionics Engineer
description_text:
Who We Are:
Venus Aerospace is revolutionizing rocket engine propulsion. With the first generational leap in rocket engine technology since Apollo, our proprietary Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine and Venus Detonation Ramjets deliver historic efficiency and performance across aerospace applications. We are building the next generation of propulsion platforms that enable breakthrough capabilities in space, defense, and commercial high-speed flight. Our team thrives on solving the toughest engineering challenges and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible in aerospace. At Venus, we’re looking for bold engineers and innovators who want to make history and redefine the future of propulsion.

The role: Senior Avionics Engineer
The Senior Avionics Engineer will lead the design, integration, and validation of avionics systems for advanced aerospace platforms. This role focuses on flight computer hardware design and selection, hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) development, interfaces between flight components, and close collaboration with external vendors and suppliers. The ideal candidate brings deep hands-on experience with aerospace hardware and the ability to guide systems from concept through flight test and production.
\n

What You'll Get To Do
Lead the architecture, design, and selection of avionics hardware, including flight computers, sensors, power systems, and communication interfaces.
Define and implement interfaces between flight components (e.g., flight computers, sensors, actuators, power systems, radios).
Develop and maintain hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test environments to support software development, integration, and verification.
Own battery sizing and power system analysis, including load analysis, margins, thermal considerations, and mission profiles.
Support system integration, ground testing, and flight testing, including troubleshooting and root cause analysis.
Collaborate with external vendors and suppliers to define requirements, evaluate components, manage technical trade studies, and support procurement.
Author and review technical documentation, including requirements, interface control documents (ICDs), schematics, test plans, and verification reports.
Ensure avionics designs meet aerospace standards, reliability, safety, and environmental requirements.
Mentor junior engineers and provide technical leadership across avionics and flight systems teams.
Collaborate with GN&C, Aerodynamics, and Structures engineering disciplines to ascertain system requirements and meet technical program needs

What You Have
Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or related field (Master’s preferred).
5+ years of experience in avionics or aerospace electrical systems engineering.
Proven experience with flight computer hardware design and/or selection.
Strong background in hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing and integration.
Hands-on experience defining and implementing interfaces between flight components.
Demonstrated experience with battery sizing, power budgets, and power system design.
Experience working with outside vendors, including technical evaluations and supplier coordination.
Proficiency reading and creating schematics, wiring diagrams, and interface documentation.
Strong troubleshooting and systems-level thinking skills.
Key Competencies: systems-level avionics expertise; cross-functional collaboration; vendor and supplier management; technical leadership and mentorship; clear technical communication

What Will Set You Apart
Experience with DO-160, DO-254, DO-178, or similar aerospace standards.
Familiarity with embedded systems, buses and protocols (CAN, UART, SPI, I2C, Ethernet, MIL-STD-1553, etc.).
Experience supporting flight test campaigns.
Background in UAV, space systems, eVTOL, missiles, launch vehicles, or experimental aircraft.
Experience with environmental testing (vibration, thermal, EMI/EMC).

\n
Location:
On-site in Houston, TX

Benefits:
Venus Aerospace employee benefits include a 401(k) plan, stock options, and medical benefits, including dental and vision. We have a flex-Friday flexible schedule, unlimited PTO, and receive professional development opportunities.

About Venus Aerospace
By enabling one hour global travel, Venus Aerospace is transforming the world and our access to it. We’re building a spaceplane with a low carbon footprint that will enable one-hour global travel. We are a diverse, veteran team with extreme experience in successful new space, commercial, R&D, and government/military programs.
We are looking for incredible, motivated, enthusiastic people that want to build a world-changing culture and organization. You will be helping Venus grow and develop, so flexibility and a sense of adventure is quintessential. We are looking for all-around AWESOME people to help us grow.
We’ve done the professional grind, and we’ve got a better approach. We take “Home for Dinner” seriously – we want you to work for us and be “home for dinner”. Family is important and we don’t want you to miss out on what matters most. If that appeals to you, we’d love to connect!

title: Trendyol Express - Dağıtım Merkezi Operasyon Yöneticisi - Esenyurt International
description_text:
Ekip Hakkında
Trendyol Express ekibi olarak amacımız, her siparişte müşterilerimize kusursuz bir teslimat deneyimi sunmaktır. Müşteriyi her zaman odağımıza alarak; en ileri teknolojiyi, esnek operasyonel altyapıyı ve yenilikçi çözümleri bir araya getiriyoruz.

Hızlı ve iş birliğine dayalı çalışma kültürümüz sayesinde; veri, çeviklik ve güçlü ekip ruhu ile her zorluğu fırsata dönüştürüyoruz. Bölgesel olarak büyürken, lojistikte yeni standartlar belirliyor ve attığımız her adımda kalıcı değer yaratmak için çalışıyoruz.

Pozisyon Hakkında
Trendyol Express’te Dağıtım Operasyon Yöneticisi olarak, en iyi uygulamalar ve yüksek kalite standartları doğrultusunda dağıtım operasyonlarının uçtan uca yönetilmesini sağlayacaksınız. Ekip aracılığıyla operasyonun tüm süreçlerinin kurumsal ve yasal gerekliliklere uygun şekilde ilerlemesini güvence altına alacak, planlama ve operasyonel parametreleri yakından takip ederek hedeflenen verimliliğin sağlanmasına katkıda bulunacaksınız. Aynı zamanda ekip motivasyonunu destekleyerek yetkinlik gelişimini teşvik edecek ve sürekli iyileştirme yaklaşımıyla operasyonel performansı artıracaksınız.

\n

Sorumluluklar
Sorumlusu olunan bölümün işleyişini takip ederek iyileştirilmesi için çalışmalar yapacak
En iyi uygulamalara ve en yüksek standartlara uymak ve bu standartların sürekli olarak elde edilmesini, gözden geçirilmesini ve geliştirilmesini sağlamak
Ekip aracılığıyla, bölümün tüm yönlerinin tanımlanmış kurumsal ve yasal gereksinimler dahilinde çalışmasını sağlamak
Operasyonel süreç dahilindeki bütün parametrelerin planlama konularının kontrolü yapmak 
Planlanan hedeflere göre operasyonun işleyişini sağlayıp verimliliği arttırmak
Ekip motivasyonunu koruyup ekibin yetkinliklerini arttırmak

Beklenen Nitelikler
Üniversitelerin tercihen mühendislik bölümlerinden mezun
Nakliye ve / veya lojistik sektöründe en az 3 yıllık yönetim deneyimi
Tedarik Zinciri bölümü ve çok modlu taşımacılık operasyonlarında deneyim (Kara taşımacılığı) yönetimi deneyimi
Operasyon, depolama, ve personel yönetimi konusunda tecrübe sahibi
Ms office programlarını iyi derecede kullanabilen
Planlama ve organizasyon yeteneği güçlü
Yazılı ve sözlü iletişimi güçlü
Analitik düşünebilen
Ekip yönetimi konusunda tecrübeli ve takım çalışmasına yatkın
B sınıfı sürücü belgesine sahip(aktif araç kullanabilen)
Askerlik ile ilişiği olmayan(erkek adaylar için)
Vardiyalı çalışabilecek
İstanbul Avrupa Yakasın'da ikamet eden/edebilecek olan

\n


Trendyol’da Sizi Neler Bekliyor?
- Hibrit Çalışma Modeli: Zamanını ve enerjini yönetirken ekibinle bağlantıda kalabileceğin esnek bir yapı. Yıl içinde yurtdışından çalışma ve yaza özel çalışma modeli gibi uygulamalarla sana en uygun dengeyi bulabilirsin.
- FlexBenefits: Günlük yemek bütçeni özelleştirebilir, sağlık sigortanı upgrade edebilir, akaryakıt desteği veya Trendyol alışveriş bakiyesi gibi ek haklar arasından ihtiyacına göre seçim yapabilirsin.
- İyi yaşam desteği: Lokasyon bazlı kurum içi doktorlarımızdan, psikolog ve diyetisyen desteğinden ve HPV aşısı kapsamından faydalanabilirsin.
- Kişiselleştirilmiş öğrenme ve gelişim fırsatları: Yıllık eğitim bütçeni ihtiyacana uygun olduğunu düşündüğün eğitim veya konferans için kullanabilirsin. LMS platformumuzdan dilediğin an faydalanabilir, yıl boyu sunulan yüz yüze eğitimlere katılabilirsin.
- İlk günden sorumluluk alma kültürü: Trendyol’da her ses duyulur ve değer görür. Sen de daha ilk günden itibaren inisiyatif alarak etki yaratabilirsin.
- Tek ruhlu, çok kültürlü bir ekip: Berlin’den Dubai’ye, Amsterdam’dan İstanbul’a... Farklı bakış açılarını bir araya getiren, start-up ruhuyla çalışan uluslararası bir ekibiz.
- En iyilerle birlikte gelişme imkânı: Anlamlı işlerde sorumluluk alabilir, deneyimleyerek öğrenebilirsin. Farklı ülkelerdeki ekip arkadaşlarımızdan mentorluk alabilir ve alanında uzman ekip arkadaşlarımızla birlikte kendini geliştirebilirsin.
- İş dışında da anlamlı bağlar: Ekip ritüelleri, etkinlikler ve sosyal aktiviteler sayesinde yalnızca birlikte çalışmıyor; birlikte gülüyor, öğreniyor ve ilham alıyoruz.

Kariyerinde Bir Sonraki Adıma Geçmek İstersen;
Bu rol seni heyecanlandırdıysa hemen başvur, bir sonraki adımı birlikte atalım.
Ekibi daha yakından tanımak mı istiyorsun?
Kariyer Sitemizi, LinkedIn sayfamızı veya YouTube kanalımızı ziyaret ederek #LifeatTrendyol ve çalışma şeklimiz hakkında daha fazla bilgi edinebilirsin.
